hero_title: Data Scientist specialized in AI and NLP
hero_description: Data Scientist, exploring, building, and connecting ideas from data to cloud
about_intro: Final-year Data Science student at ENSAI, with a strong background in mathematics, statistics, and machine learning.
about_text: A Data Science graduate (ENSAI), I have developed strong skills in designing end-to-end Machine Learning and data pipelines on the cloud, including the deployment of ML APIs in Python. I have expertise in statistics, machine learning, deep learning, as well as NLP, Computer Vision, and Generative AI, with a particular focus on model evaluation and post-processing. I am looking for a Data Scientist opportunity to contribute to applied AI and industrialization projects, combining analytical rigor with business impact.
edu1_school: ENSAI – National School for Statistics and Information Analysis (Ker Lann, Rennes)
edu1_desc: Grande École under the INSEE – Top-tier program in statistics & AI
edu1_course1: Key courses: Supervised & unsupervised learning, deep learning (PyTorch, TensorFlow), NLP with Transformers, generative AI (LLMs, RAG), time series, Bayesian inference, stochastic calculus
edu1_course2: Big Data & Engineering: Spark, Hadoop, SQL, APIs, Docker, MLOps, cloud deployment
edu1_course3: Applied work: Real-world projects in fraud detection, recommendation systems, and NLP
projects_subtitle: A selection of my end-to-end data & AI projects
project1_desc: Fully local pipeline for moderating user reviews: toxicity detection, explainability, and performance evaluation, privacy-friendly and cloud-free.
project1_desc_long:
ReviewGuardian is a privacy-focused NLP system for identifying and managing toxic content in user reviews. It includes:

  • End-to-end review pipeline: scraping, preprocessing, and custom text cleaning
  • Multi-model toxicity detection using Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, and Random Forest with TF-IDF
  • Explainability layer powered by SHAP for local interpretation of predictions
  • Model versioning and evaluation with built-in A/B testing and performance tracking
  • Fully local deployment: Streamlit front-end and FastAPI backend, no cloud, full control

All inference runs locally to guarantee complete data privacy and maximum transparency.
project2_desc: Intelligently recommends tracks using Word2Vec and NLP embeddings. Fully local app with interactive filters and playlist export.
project2_desc_long:
This hybrid music recommender system combines two powerful engines: (1) collaborative filtering based on Word2Vec (co-listening patterns), and (2) semantic similarity using Sentence-BERT embeddings. The application offers:
  • Dual similarity engine: co-occurrence (Word2Vec) + semantic matching (Sentence-BERT)
  • Interactive filters for genre, duration, mood, and decade
  • Exploration mode with audio features (energy, danceability, positivity)
  • Real-time KPIs: 94.2% precision, 85ms latency, 4.6/5 user rating
  • Advanced dashboard with heatmaps, radar charts, and Spotify benchmarks
  • Smart playlist generator with CSV export and feedback integration
  • Fully local deployment, no cloud, no API dependency
Built with performance, explainability, and personalization in mind, a powerful sandbox for experimenting with music recommender systems.
project3_desc: Hybrid approach (Machine Learning + Statistical Rules) optimizing Cross-Sell. Streamlit application with dashboards and market simulation.
project3_desc_long:
This project addresses the Zimnat Insurance Recommendation challenge (Zindi): predicting a client's missing products using a snapshot of their current portfolio.
  • Hybrid Approach (Dual Engine): Combines a Statistical Baseline (Bayesian) for robustness with a CatBoost model for fine-grained personalization.
  • Anti-Cheat Filter: Strict business rules ensuring no already-owned products are recommended (zero commercial errors).
  • Modular Streamlit App: 5 dedicated interfaces (Executive Dashboard, Business Insights, Client Inspector for agents, Market Simulator).
  • Business Impact: Transition from Mass Marketing to Precision Marketing to maximize LTV and reduce churn.
Designed for operational efficiency, this system transforms a product list into a targeted client strategy.
skills_subtitle: Core strengths in data, ML, NLP & engineering
skills_adv_data_science_desc: Bayesian inference, econometrics, linear/GLM models, ARIMA/SARIMA, VAR, ETS, convex optimization.
skills_adv_ml_desc: Supervised & unsupervised, XGBoost, Deep Learning (CNN, LSTM), explainability (SHAP, LIME), recommender systems.
skills_adv_nlp_desc: RAG pipelines (BM25 + reranking), LLMs (Hugging Face, LangChain), embeddings, vector indices (FAISS, ChromaDB).

skills_adv_cloud_desc: Spark, Hadoop, AWS (EC2), GCP (Cloud Run), scalable pipeline management and cloud architecture.
skills_adv_viz_desc: Matplotlib, Seaborn, Plotly, interactive dashboards, distilling complex models for decision-makers.
techskills_subtitle: Main technologies, libraries & tools I use daily
exp1_title: Square Management - Applied Research Intern (GenAI & RAG)
exp1_desc: We joined the R&D Lab to tackle a major challenge: unlocking the value of unexploited strategic documents (Finance, CSR). These documents, often long and complex, were difficult to query. Our mission was to build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system capable not only of retrieving information but also ensuring total traceability for consultants.
exp1_task1: We conducted a rigorous comparative study of all technical components (from OCR to LLMs) to isolate the perfect combination for each document type.
exp1_task2: We designed a 'Document-Centric' architecture that respects the native structure of reports (tables, summaries) to preserve every semantic nuance.
exp1_task3: Finally, we delivered a reproducible experimental framework with over 150 tested configurations, scientifically measuring the impact of each choice on response quality.
exp1_task4: Stack: Python, LangChain, Hugging Face, FAISS, Azure OpenAI, Ragas.
exp2_title: City of Paris - Data Scientist (Mobility & Environment)
exp2_desc: We joined the Roads Department for a mission with high public impact: enlightening sustainable mobility policies with data. The goal was to precisely identify which company fleets contribute most to urban pollution to better target city actions.
exp2_task1: We built a massive data pipeline cross-referencing scattered sources (registrations, public registries, traffic data) while ensuring intensive cleaning to guarantee diagnostic reliability.
exp2_task2: We developed an Explainable Composite Pollution Score, factoring in every technical characteristic (engine type, age, critically) to segment economic actors.
exp2_task3: To finish, we transformed these analyses into decision-support tools (dashboards, reports) enabling policymakers to prioritize the most effective levers for action.
exp2_task4: Stack: Python, Pandas, Clustering, REST API, Data Visualization.
contact_text: I'm always open to new opportunities and collaborations. Drop me a message!
contact_success: Message sent successfully! I'll get back to you soon.
bf_section_challenge_text: A broker receives dozens of credit applications per week. Each file contains structured data (income, debt, history), a free-form note, and sometimes missing or contradictory documents. Two costly mistakes: accepting a risky file or refusing a good client. A score alone is not enough: you need a clear, justified, and defensible decision.
bf_section_solution_intro: BrokerFlow AI combines three complementary layers:
bf_section_solution_list1: Calibrated ML model to estimate default probability from structured data
bf_section_solution_list2: Deterministic policy that translates this score into a business decision, considering file completeness and alert severity
bf_section_solution_list3: Lightweight agents that read the free note, check file consistency, and write a readable explanation for the broker
bf_section_solution_outro: A file enters the API. It comes out as ACCEPTABLE, REVIEW, REQUEST_DOCUMENTS or ESCALATE — with reason codes and a natural language summary.
project5_title: Retail Demand Forecasting & Inventory Optimization Assistant
project5_desc: Comprehensive AI solution for retail: demand forecasting, uncertainty quantification, and inventory optimization, based on real data and a robust hybrid strategy.
retail_title: Retail Demand Forecasting & Inventory Optimization Assistant
retail_subtitle: Don't just predict sales. Optimize decisions.
retail_challenge_desc: In retail, every store manager faces a critical weekly question: 'How much should I order?' ...
retail_understock: Understock: Lost sales, dissatisfied customers, damaged brand image.
retail_overstock: Overstock: Tied-up cash, storage costs, risk of loss (perishables) or shrinkage.
retail_env_promo: Promotional volatility: Massive promotions create sudden demand spikes.
retail_env_calendar: Complex calendar: Local, regional, and national holidays, often shifted.
retail_env_macro: Macroeconomic factors: Sensitivity to oil prices.
retail_env_diversity: Product diversity: Thousands of SKUs, from staples to niche products.
retail_obj1: Forecast weekly demand over an 8-week horizon.
retail_obj2: Quantify the uncertainty of this forecast.
retail_obj3: Recommend an optimal order quantity based on economic costs.
retail_etl_cartesian: Cartesian grid: Generate missing rows to distinguish 'No sale' from 'No demand'.
retail_etl_iso: ISO Weekly Aggregation: Use of ISO 8601 standard.
retail_etl_parquet: Parquet output: Cleaned data stored in data/processed/*.parquet.
retail_etl_forecasts: forecasts.sqlite: Probabilistic forecasts and run registry.
retail_baseline_desc: The project's 'Baseline' is not a single model, but a Smart Hybrid Strategy (Smart Routing)...
retail_baseline_diag: Diagnosis (Product Analysis): Calculation of ADI and CV² indicators.
retail_baseline_class: Classification (Syntetos-Boylan Matrix): Product classification by ADI and CV².
retail_baseline_routing: Routing (Model Choice): Apply the best formula for each profile.
retail_table_smooth_why: Smooths noise while retaining some seasonality memory.
retail_table_erratic_why: High volatility: rely more on seasonality.
retail_table_rare_why: Ensures the 'minimum vital' to avoid overstocking.
retail_baseline_summary: The baseline aims to be statistically robust, avoiding classic modeling errors.
retail_decision_p10: P10 (Low Scenario): 90% sure to sell at least this much.
retail_decision_p90: P90 (High Scenario): 10% chance to sell more than this.
retail_decision_newsvendor1: If the cost of understock is high, the system targets a high quantile (e.g., P80).
retail_decision_newsvendor2: If the cost of overstock is high, the system targets a low quantile (e.g., P40).
